name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: Crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers in case of crop failure due to natural calamities, pests & diseases.
benefit: Up to ₹2 lakh insurance coverage per farmer
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to small and marginal farmer families.
eligibility: Farmers with landholding up to 2 hectares

name: Rythu Bharosa (AP)
description: Andhra Pradesh government scheme providing investment support to farmers at the start of each crop season.
eligibility: All farmer families in Andhra Pradesh owning cultivable land
deadline: Ongoing — register at nearest agriculture office

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Provides short-term credit to farmers for crop cultivation, post-harvest expenses, and allied activities.
benefit: Credit limit up to ₹3 lakh at 4% interest rate
deadline: Apply at any nationalized bank / cooperative bank

name: National Mission on Oilseeds & Oil Palm
description: Support for cultivation of oilseeds and oil palm with subsidies on seeds, fertilizers, and farm equipment.
benefit: Subsidy up to 50% on certified seeds and inputs
eligibility: Farmers in AP growing sunflower, groundnut, sesame, oil palm
deadline: Before sowing season — contact local agriculture officer

name: Sub Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
description: Subsidy scheme for purchasing modern farm equipment like tractors, harvesters, and irrigation equipment.
benefit: 40–50% subsidy on farm machinery for small farmers
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers, women farmers get priority

name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana (PMKSY)
description: Promotes efficient water use and expands irrigation coverage with focus on drip and sprinkler irrigation.
benefit: 55% subsidy for drip irrigation, 45% for sprinklers
eligibility: All farmers, SCs/STs get higher subsidy

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
description: Promotes organic farming by providing financial assistance for cluster formation, training, and certification.
eligibility: Farmers willing to adopt organic farming practices
